translations.intro2: Timeless Stories of El Salvador V2: Epiphany
translations.introSM: Timeless Stories of El Salvador — Spanish Mirrors
translations.intro3: is the 1st book of its kind: one of 10 mirroring legends that place Salvadoran and Spanish stories side by side, revealing how distant cultures reflect the same symbols and fears.
translations.launchHeroBefore: On May 1st, Spanish Mirrors premieres at SIEL 2026 in Rabat, UNESCO World Book Capital. Join the journey.
translations.launchHeroAfter: Presented at SIEL 2026 in Rabat, UNESCO World Book Capital, Spanish Mirrors is the 1st book of its kind: 10 mirrored legends revealing how distant cultures reflect the same symbols and fears.
translations.author1: is a Spanish–Salvadoran technology strategist and cultural researcher dedicated to building bridges across nations through the timeless power of folklore.
translations.author2: Although his professional background is in global IT strategy, Federico’s path as a storyteller emerged while living as an immigrant in Poland, Spain, and Luxembourg. Traveling across more than twenty countries, he encountered simplified views of his homeland, yet found that Salvadoran legends resonated with universal depth. Discovering a total absence of English-language resources for these stories, he created the first series of Salvadoran folklore books designed specifically for the international stage.
translations.author3: What began as cultural preservation evolved into “Spanish Mirrors,” a comparative study that places Salvadoran, Spanish, and—most recently—North African legends in direct dialogue. By pairing figures like the Siguanaba with the Midnight Washerwomen or Morocco’s Aisha Qandisha, Federico examines the “human code”: the shared archetypes and moral functions that emerge whenever societies give form to the unknown.
translations.author31: Today, his work serves as a vital cultural bridge, officially preserved in National Archives and recognized for its diplomatic value. His research has been featured at global cultural summits and presented at Embassies and Instituto Cervantes centers across three continents, including landmark sessions in Austria, Australia, China (online), India, Turkey, and Morocco.
translations.author311: In 2026, his mission reached a new pinnacle with the official global launch of his third volume at the SIEL International Book Fair in Rabat, during its historic year as the UNESCO World Book Capital.
translations.author4: He often says: “The only person who can reshape your destiny is the one you see in the mirror.” Through these stories, he invites readers to see not just El Salvador — but themselves — in a whole new light.

siguanaba.name: The Siguanaba
siguanaba.desc: A female spirit that scares men.

goodAndBadCadejo.desc: A pair of beasts that protect or scare people at night.

mulus.desc: A beast that lives in the cemeteries.

fairJudgeOfTheNight.desc: A judge that appears at night to punish the guilty.

weepingWoman.name: The Weeping Woman
weepingWoman.desc: A woman that cries in the cemeteries.

headlessPriest.name: The Headless Priest
headlessPriest.desc: A priest that was decapitated and now roams the streets.

fleshlessWoman.name: The Fleshless Woman
fleshlessWoman.desc: A female spirit that scares men in the streets at night.

dwarf.name: The Dwarf
dwarf.desc: A mischievous being that bothers pretty girls.

caveOfSalamanca.desc: A professor that teaches dark arts.
